title: Roche Restarts Phase III Trial for Elevidys to Secure EMA Approval
summary: New Delhi: Roche on Thursday said it is initiating a late stage study for its gene therapy ‚ÄãElevidys following feedback from the European drug regulator. The ‚Äåtherapy for duchenne muscular dystrophy...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
New Delhi: Roche on Thursday said it is initiating a late stage study for its gene therapy ‚ÄãElevidys following feedback from the European drug regulator. The ‚Äåtherapy for duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD), for which Roche partners with American Sarepta Therapeutics had failed to win backing from ‚Äãthe European Medicines Agency (EMA) last year after ‚Äãcoming under scrutiny following two patient deaths tied to ‚ÅÝthe treatment.

  • The phase III study aims to generate ‚Äãadditional placebo-controlled data required for a regulatory re-submission with ‚ÄãEMA.
  • Elevidys is currently approved in nine countries for ambulatory boys with a confirmed DMD mutation, including in the United States, ‚Äãwhich had temporarily ordered a pause in shipments last ‚ÄãJuly.
  • DMD is a rare, inherited disease that mostly affects boys ‚Äåand ‚ÅÝcauses muscles to weaken steadily over time.
  • The study will span more than 72 weeks and evaluate the efficacy and safety of Elevidys compared to placebo in ‚Äãaround 100 ‚Äãearly ambulatory ‚ÅÝboys with DMD. The primary endpoint is the change in the so-called "time to ‚Äãrise" from the floor velocity.
  • "Our confidence is ‚Äãrooted ‚ÅÝin robust long-term data showing the durable efficacy and safety of Elevidys, alongside the experience of treating more ‚ÅÝthan ‚Äãa thousand ambulatory boys worldwide," ‚ÄãRoche's Chief Medical Officer and Head of Global Product Development Levi ‚ÄãGarraway said.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
